observed: A deterministic adapter now maps exactly one gh attestation verification result into the Cloudflare signer-authorization input schema.
The adapter extracts repository, workflow path, git ref, and a normalized SHA-256 certificate fingerprint from verifier-authenticated certificate fields.
The adapter fails closed when attestation cardinality, verified timestamps, certificate identity, fingerprint, or policy are absent.
Four deterministic Node tests were committed for success, missing fingerprint, missing workflow identity, and ambiguous cardinality.
inferred: Once wired into the deployment workflow, signer authorization can be based on exact authenticated certificate identity rather than workflow-controlled statement fields alone.
limits: No GitHub Actions run exists yet for commit c488b7c0f2ab21f48d5c43167802b8ab94567a08.
The local execution environment could not resolve github.com, so the committed test was not independently executed in this cycle.
The exact certificate field names emitted by the installed gh version must be confirmed from a real workflow artifact before acceptance is enabled.
Signer authorization authenticates provenance scope; it does not prove deployment success or scientific truth.
falsification_route: Run the committed test under Node in CI, capture real gh attestation verify --format=json output, and reject the adapter if authenticated repository, workflow, ref, and fingerprint cannot be recovered without consulting workflow-controlled statement fields.
next_action: Wire the adapter test and runtime invocation into cloudflare-pages-research.yml, generate a deny-by-default signer policy file, and require an authorized result in the acceptance invariant.
